root_text.sn48.48:1.2: “Katinaṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu aññaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn48.48:1.3: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti?
root_text.sn48.48:2.2: “pañcannaṁ kho, bhikkhave, indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu aññaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn48.48:2.3: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmīti.
root_text.sn48.48:2.6: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu aññaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn48.48:2.7: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn48.48:1.2: “Mendicants, how many faculties must a mendicant develop and cultivate so that they can declare enlightenment:
translation_text.sn48.48:1.3: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place”’?”
translation_text.sn48.48:2.1: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn48.48:2.2: “A mendicant must develop and cultivate five faculties so that they can declare enlightenment.
translation_text.sn48.48:2.5: The faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.48:2.6: These are the five faculties that a mendicant must develop and cultivate so that they can declare enlightenment:
translation_text.sn48.48:2.7: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place”’.”
